summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting featuring recognition of police officers, including the swearing-in of returning Officer Dave Perkins, drug interdiction awards, and Sergeant Skates as Officer of the Year. The Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association presented donations totaling over $55,000 to local organizations including the fire department. The board approved multiple personnel appointments including two full-time police officers and a part-time planning and zoning director, along with equipment purchases including a 2025 Ford cruiser and a treadmill donation from the CPAAA.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Feb 11-25, 2025) - passed
Motion to accept $325 donation from Snack Pack for Life Foundation for police and fire - passed
Motion to accept $80 in food gift cards from Heartland Christian Church for police department - passed
Motion to accept donation of treadmill and five lock boxes from CPAAA - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel to Houston, Ohio (Aug 18-20) for Officer Sites at $600 registration - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Officer Tyler Smith for Excel crash reconstructionist training at $699 - passed
Motion to approve purchase of 2025 Ford cruiser for $564 (partial amount referenced) - passed
Motion to approve revision to surplus items sale on GovDeals (removing .40 cal ammo) - passed
Motion to purchase three recycled plastic picnic tables from U-Line not to exceed $4,361.31 - passed
Motion to authorize $1,654.82 payment to Goshen Township Historical Society for tornado-damaged garage - passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:06 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1) - passed
Motion to appoint Chris Whitaker as full-time police officer - passed
Motion to appoint Jason Franklin as full-time police officer upon pre-employment screening - passed
Motion to appoint John Langenheim as part-time planning and zoning director at $28/hour - passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:44 PM - passed

$45,732 - Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association donation to Goshen Township Fire Department
$325 - Snack Pack for Life Foundation donation for police and fire
$80 - Heartland Christian Church food gift cards for police

notable_topics: Snow removal season costs and statistics update
Congressman David Taylor's office community outreach and services
Swearing-in of Officer Dave Perkins returning to Goshen Township PD
2024 Drug Interdiction Awards for Sergeant Collier and Sergeant Houck
Sergeant Skates recognized as 2024 Officer of the Year
Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association dissolution and donation distributions
CPAAA donation of treadmill and gun lock boxes
Purchase of 2025 Ford cruiser to replace 2017 patrol unit
Revision to surplus items sale regarding .40 caliber ammunition retention
Goshen Township Historical Society tornado damage repair funding
Appointment of two new full-time police officers (Whitaker and Franklin)
Appointment of part-time planning and zoning director (Langenheim)
Fire station construction progress update - detention work and foundation
Commendation of firefighter paramedic Harley for compassionate service
attendees: Joseph Abner (Deputy District Director, Congressman David Taylor's office)
Steve Greer (Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association)
notes: The Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association has dissolved as an organization and distributed its remaining funds to local organizations. The fire station construction project is progressing with Conger delivering a trailer and starting detention/stormwater work. The police department is currently down three full-time officers. The meeting included a recess for refreshments from approximately 6:55 PM to 7:06 PM. The F2 tornado referenced occurred on July 6, 2022. The 2025 Ford cruiser exact total cost was unclear from transcript but referenced $564 which may be partial. Officer Perkins previously served at Goshen Township PD for nine years before spending 22 years at Union Township PD.
title: BOT 2-25-25
